017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.validation;
018
019import java.util.*;
020import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
021import java.util.logging.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
024import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
025
026import jakarta.validation.*;
027
028/**
029 * Static dispatcher for the optional Jakarta Bean Validation 3.x integration.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Acts as a thin shim between Juneau's REST argument resolvers ({@code ContentArg}, {@code FormDataArg},
033 * {@code RequestBeanArg}) and the {@code jakarta.validation.Validator} engine. Per the package-level contract
034 * (see {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.validation package overview}), <b>validation is off by default and never
035 * runs automatically</b>: callers query {@link #isValidationRequested(ParameterInfo)} once at arg-resolver
036 * construction time, then conditionally invoke {@link #validate(Object, BeanStore)} per request only when an
037 * explicit {@code @jakarta.validation.Valid} (or equivalent) marker is present.
038 *
039 * <h5 class='topic'>Validator resolution order</h5>
040 * <ol>
041 *    <li>A user-supplied {@code Validator} bean visible from the supplied {@link BeanStore} &mdash; typically
042 *       contributed via a {@code @Bean public Validator validator()} factory on a {@code @Rest}-annotated
043 *       resource. This is the recommended path when the application needs custom message interpolators,
044 *       group sequences, or constraint-validator factories.
045 *    <li>A lazily-built JVM-wide default obtained from
046 *       {@link Validation#buildDefaultValidatorFactory()}. Cached after the first successful build so the
047 *       factory cost is paid at most once per JVM.
048 *    <li>{@code null} &mdash; signals graceful degradation when neither a bean-store entry nor a runtime
049 *       provider is available. {@link #validate(Object, BeanStore)} returns the input bean unchanged in this
050 *       case and logs a single {@code WARNING} per JVM so misconfigured deployments are visible without
051 *       flooding the log.
052 * </ol>
053 *
054 * <h5 class='topic'>Why static dispatch?</h5>
055 * <p>
056 * Each REST argument resolver is built once per {@code @RestOp} method at servlet startup, then invoked on
057 * every request. By keeping the validator lookup off the arg-resolver constructor and inside this class's
058 * cached static accessor, the cost of locating the {@code Validator} is paid once per JVM rather than once
059 * per request &mdash; and resolvers for {@code @RestOp} methods that never opt in pay nothing at all (the
060 * static initializer doesn't run until the first call to {@link #validate(Object, BeanStore)}).
061 *
062 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ValidationException}
064 *    <li class='jc'>{@link ValidationViolation}
065 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerValidation">REST Server &mdash; Jakarta Validation</a>
066 * </ul>
067 *
068 * @since 10.0.0
069 */
070public final class BeanValidator {
071
072   private static final Logger LOG = Logger.getLogger(BeanValidator.class.getName());
073
074   /**
075    * Holds the lazily-resolved JVM-wide default {@link Validator} (built once on first need from
076    * {@link Validation#buildDefaultValidatorFactory()}). {@code null} until first successful build.
077    */
078   private static final AtomicReference<Validator> DEFAULT_VALIDATOR = new AtomicReference<>();
079
080   /**
081    * Flips to {@code true} once we've discovered &mdash; via either a {@link ValidationException} from the
082    * provider lookup or a {@link Throwable} from {@link Validation#buildDefaultValidatorFactory()} &mdash;
083    * that no runtime validator engine is reachable on this classpath. Short-circuits subsequent calls so we
084    * don't repeatedly pay the failed-lookup cost.
085    */
086   private static final AtomicBoolean DEFAULT_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE = new AtomicBoolean();
087
088   /** One-shot guard so the &quot;no provider on classpath&quot; warning only logs once per JVM. */
089   private static final AtomicBoolean MISSING_PROVIDER_LOGGED = new AtomicBoolean();
090
091   private BeanValidator() {}
092
093   /**
094    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if the supplied parameter carries any of the recognized validation opt-in
095    * markers (currently {@code jakarta.validation.Valid}, {@code javax.validation.Valid}, or Spring's
096    * {@code @Validated}).
097    *
098    * <p>
099    * Detection is purely FQN-based via {@link JsrSupport#isValidAnnotation(AnnotationInfo)} &mdash; no
100    * compile-time dependency on the Jakarta Validation or Spring annotation classes themselves is required
101    * to recognize the marker.
102    *
103    * <p>
104    * Arg resolvers should call this exactly once at construction time and cache the result; the per-request
105    * hot path then becomes a single boolean check before deciding whether to call
106    * {@link #validate(Object, BeanStore)}.
107    *
108    * @param paramInfo The Java method parameter being inspected. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
109    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the parameter has been opted into validation.
110    */
111   public static boolean isValidationRequested(ParameterInfo paramInfo) {
112      return paramInfo.getAnnotations().stream().anyMatch(JsrSupport::isValidAnnotation);
113   }
114
115   /**
116    * Runs Jakarta Bean Validation against the supplied bean and throws a {@link ValidationException} if any
117    * constraints are violated.
118    *
119    * <p>
120    * Resolution order for the {@link Validator} is described on the class javadoc. When no validator can
121    * be obtained (no bean-store entry, no runtime provider), this method logs a one-shot warning and
122    * returns {@code bean} unchanged &mdash; preserving the off-by-default contract even when an arg
123    * resolver was constructed with {@code @Valid} but the deployment forgot to ship a provider.
124    *
125    * <p>
126    * A {@code null} {@code bean} returns {@code null} without invoking the validator (Jakarta's
127    * {@code validator.validate(null)} throws {@code IllegalArgumentException}, which would just produce an
128    * unhelpful 500 instead of the natural &quot;missing body&quot; 400 the upstream arg resolver already
129    * surfaces).
130    *
131    * @param <T> The bean type being validated.
132    * @param bean The resolved bean to validate. May be <jk>null</jk>.
133    * @param beanStore The op-session bean store consulted for a user-supplied {@link Validator}. May be
134    *    <jk>null</jk> (treated as &quot;no user override&quot;).
135    * @return The input {@code bean} (unchanged).
136    * @throws ValidationException If one or more constraints are violated.
137    */
138   public static <T> T validate(T bean, BeanStore beanStore) {
139      if (bean == null)
140         return null;
141      var validator = resolveValidator(beanStore);
142      if (validator == null) {
143         warnMissingProviderOnce();
144         return bean;
145      }
146      var violations = validator.validate(bean);
147      if (violations.isEmpty())
148         return bean;
149      throw new ValidationException(toViolationList(violations));
150   }
151
152   /**
153    * Test/diagnostic hook &mdash; resets the JVM-wide default-validator cache so the next call to
154    * {@link #validate(Object, BeanStore)} re-resolves the provider. Not part of the public contract;
155    * intended for unit tests that simulate provider-missing scenarios.
156    */
157   public static void resetCachedDefaultForTesting() {
158      DEFAULT_VALIDATOR.set(null);
159      DEFAULT_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE.set(false);
160      MISSING_PROVIDER_LOGGED.set(false);
161   }
162
163   /**
164    * Test/diagnostic hook &mdash; forces the &quot;no provider on classpath&quot; code path. Not part of
165    * the public contract; intended for unit tests that need to assert the graceful-degradation behavior
166    * without actually removing the Hibernate Validator JAR from the test classpath.
167    *
168    * <p>
169    * After calling this, {@link #validate(Object, BeanStore)} will behave as if no Jakarta Bean
170    * Validation provider is reachable: it short-circuits to a no-op and emits the one-shot
171    * &quot;missing provider&quot; warning. Call {@link #resetCachedDefaultForTesting()} afterwards to
172    * restore normal lookup.
173    */
174   public static void simulateProviderMissingForTesting() {
175      DEFAULT_VALIDATOR.set(null);
176      DEFAULT_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE.set(true);
177      MISSING_PROVIDER_LOGGED.set(false);
178   }
179
180   private static Validator resolveValidator(BeanStore beanStore) {
181      if (beanStore != null) {
182         var fromStore = beanStore.getBean(Validator.class).orElse(null);
183         if (fromStore != null)
184            return fromStore;
185      }
186      return getDefaultValidator();
187   }
188
189   @SuppressWarnings({
190      "resource" // ValidatorFactory is intentionally not closed; it is cached for the process lifetime.
191   })
192   private static Validator getDefaultValidator() {
193      if (DEFAULT_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE.get())
194         return null;
195      var cached = DEFAULT_VALIDATOR.get();
196      if (cached != null)
197         return cached;
198      try {
199         var built = Validation.buildDefaultValidatorFactory().getValidator();
200         DEFAULT_VALIDATOR.compareAndSet(null, built);
201         return DEFAULT_VALIDATOR.get();
202      } catch (Throwable t) {  // NOSONAR: ValidationException, NoClassDefFoundError, LinkageError all in scope here.
203         DEFAULT_PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE.set(true);
204         return null;
205      }
206   }
207
208   private static void warnMissingProviderOnce() {
209      if (MISSING_PROVIDER_LOGGED.compareAndSet(false, true)) {
210         LOG.log(Level.WARNING,
211            "Jakarta Bean Validation was requested via @Valid on a REST argument, but no jakarta.validation.Validator " +
212            "bean is registered and no runtime provider (e.g. org.hibernate.validator:hibernate-validator + " +
213            "jakarta.el implementation) is on the classpath. Validation will be silently skipped for this and " +
214            "subsequent requests. Add a provider dependency or register a Validator bean to enable validation.");
215      }
216   }
217
218   @SuppressWarnings({
219      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity is marginally over (16) due to null-guard ternaries and the stable-ordering comparator; the validation payload shape is asserted by tests, so the logic is kept inline rather than fragmented.
220   })
221   private static List<ValidationViolation> toViolationList(Set<? extends ConstraintViolation<?>> violations) {
222      var result = new ArrayList<ValidationViolation>(violations.size());
223      for (var cv : violations) {
224         result.add(new ValidationViolation(
225            cv.getPropertyPath() == null ? null : cv.getPropertyPath().toString(),
226            cv.getMessage(),
227            cv.getConstraintDescriptor() == null || cv.getConstraintDescriptor().getAnnotation() == null
228               ? null
229               : cv.getConstraintDescriptor().getAnnotation().annotationType().getSimpleName()
230         ));
231      }
232      // Stable ordering — Jakarta returns a Set, but tests assert on payload shape so we sort by path+message.
233      result.sort((a, b) -> {
234         var ap = a.getPath() == null ? "" : a.getPath();
235         var bp = b.getPath() == null ? "" : b.getPath();
236         var c = ap.compareTo(bp);
237         if (c != 0)
238            return c;
239         var am = a.getMessage() == null ? "" : a.getMessage();
240         var bm = b.getMessage() == null ? "" : b.getMessage();
241         return am.compareTo(bm);
242      });
243      return result;
244   }
245}
